name: Regional Transportation District
overview: The Regional Transportation District is a regional public transit agency operating bus, light rail, and commuter rail services across the Font Range from Lone Tree in the South to Longmont in the North.

history.title: RTD Founded
history.description: The Regional Transportation District was founded in 1969 and was entirely bus-focused.

history.title: Central Corridor Opens
history.description: The Central Corridor (now D line) opened as RTD's first rail line, operating between 30th & Downing and I-25 & Broadway Stations

history.title: Southwest Corridor Extension
history.description: The Southwest Corridor Extension created a new line that stretched from I-25 & Broadway Station to Mineral Avenue Station

history.title: Central Platte Valley Extension
history.description: The Central Platte Valley extension created a new C line that connected from Union Station to I-25 & Broadway Station.

history.title: Southeast Corridor Extension
history.description: The Southeast Corridor extension created a new E line that ran from Union Station to Lincoln Station, F line that ran from 18th & California to Lincoln Station, and H line that ran from 18th & California to the Nine Mile Station.

history.title: West Rail Line Opens
history.description: The West Line, now called the W Line, was a new rail line from Jefferson County Government Center/Golden Station to Auraria West Station, continuing along existing track to Union Station. It was the first line opened under RTD's FasTracks program, which voters approved in 2004.

history.title: A Line Opens
history.description: As part of the FasTracks and Eagle P3 programs, RTD opened a series of commuter rail lines, starting with the A Line. The A Line runs from Union Station to Denver International Airport, creating a connection for travelers.

history.title: B Line Opens
history.description: The second part of the Eagle P3 program, the B line is a commuter rail line that runs from Union Station north to Westminster Station.

history.title: R Line Opens
history.description: The R line runs from Peoria Station to Lincoln Station, completing a loop with the A and D Lines.

history.title: Downtown Loop Opens
history.description: RTD Opened the downtown loop, starting with the L Line, to serve an area downtown. The loop serves from 16th Street to 30th/Downing Station.

history.title: G Line Opens
history.description: The G line is the final part of the Eagle P3 program, connecting Union Station to Arvada and Wheat Ridge, terminating at Wheat Ridge/Ward Station.
